media.base_medium: Paracoccus denitrificans defined minimal chemostat medium (succinate carbon / nitrate electron acceptor), phosphate-buffered, with 2 mL/L Vishniac & Santer trace-element solution; anaerobic denitrifying chemostat, D=0.05 h-1. High-copper variant ([Cu]~13 umol/L, full CuSO4 in trace mix).

sources_chased.what: Target paper PMC3831637 (Woolfenden et al. 2013, MicrobiologyOpen) - a modeling paper; states chemostat conditions but defers medium composition to Felgate et al. 2012
Base medium as cited to Felgate et al. 2012 (Environ Microbiol 14:1788, PMID 22642644) - primary source; full text paywalled (HTTP 403 on PNAS/Wiley)
Gaimster et al. 2016 (Front. Microbiol. 7:1806) - same Richardson/Gates/Rowley lab, reproduces the identical defined medium verbatim (base salts + full Vishniac & Santer trace-element composition)
added: full Vishniac & Santer trace metals (Zn,Mn,Fe,Mo,Cu,Co,Ca) with stock concentrations

media.notes: Recipe recovered from Gaimster et al. 2016, which reproduces verbatim the same defined P. denitrificans succinate/nitrate chemostat medium used by Felgate et al. 2012 (the base-medium reference cited by the modeling paper PMC3831637). All 12 trace/salt concentrations already in the record match a 500x (2 mL/L) dilution of the Vishniac & Santer stock exactly. The two previously uncovered compounds (Na2HPO4 buffer, EDTA chelator) are confirmed real and now included. This is the high-copper variant (full CuSO4 = 12.8 umol/L ~ the paper's 13 umol/L); the low-copper variant instead omits/chelates Cu with ascorbate+BCA. No vitamins are present (Vishniac & Santer is a trace-metal-only mix; P. denitrificans grows in this defined medium without added vitamins). No chemical reductant is added - the culture is anaerobic by nitrate respiration (denitrifying chemostat).
